title: Dendrobium wallichii
taxonomy: Dendrobium wallichii is classified under the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other members of the orchid family. Within the Orchidaceae family, Dendrobium wallichii is further categorized as a member of the genus Dendrobium, which is a diverse group of epiphytic orchids. The genus Dendrobium is itself a part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary history and relationships of Dendrobium wallichii within the orchid family.

intro: Dendrobium wallichii, also known as the 'Thorny Dendrobium', is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas, characterized by its unique, thorny pseudobulbs and delicate white or pale yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and combating infections. The bioactive constituents of Dendrobium wallichii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dendrobium wallichii is often processed into a decoction or infused into a tea, and it is also used in herbal supplements to support immune function and overall well-being.
